Company: Sirius Technology Sydney part of Sirius People Pty Ltd
Ad Text:
Senior Data Engineer | Microsoft Fabric Specialist
Location: Sydney, NSW (Preferrable) but can be Melbourne, VIC based too.
Role Type: Full-time, Permanent role

We are representing a leading Australian specialist firm that delivers high-impact financial performance and data analytics solutions across the Asia-Pacific region. Our client is looking for a Senior Data Engineer to lead the charge in designing and implementing modern data solutions using Microsoft Fabric.

The Opportunity
In this role, you will bridge the gap between complex business requirements and technical execution. You will architect end-to-end data pipelines, mentor junior engineers, and drive innovation within a highly collaborative Agile environment.

Key Responsibilities
Architect & Design: Lead the implementation of scalable data warehousing, lakehouse, and analytics solutions using Microsoft Fabric.
Develop Pipelines: Build and maintain robust data transformation processes using Python and PySpark.
Data Quality: Design monitoring frameworks to ensure the highest standards of data accuracy and reliability.
Automation & DevOps: Optimize existing pipelines and integrate CI/CD practices to streamline delivery.
Mentorship: Act as a technical lead, guiding junior team members and contributing to strategic architectural decisions.


Technical Requirements
Experience: 5+ years in Data Engineering roles.
Microsoft Fabric Expertise: Deep hands-on experience with Data Factory, Synapse Analytics, and Power BI.
Programming: Advanced Python skills (pandas, numpy, pyspark) and expert-level SQL.
Azure Ecosystem: Proven experience with Azure Synapse Analytics, including SQL and Spark pools.
Frameworks: Strong understanding of dimensional modeling and Agile/Scrum methodologies.
Preferred: Knowledge of Databricks, Delta Lake, or Generative AI/LLM integration.
Why Join This Team?
Our client prides itself on a collegiate culture that balances professional excellence with an enjoyable, supportive work environment.

Flexible Work: Enjoy flexible arrangements to maintain a healthy work-life balance.
Professional Growth: Access to ongoing learning, development opportunities, and exposure to cutting-edge Microsoft technologies.
Impact: Work on some of the largest and most successful IT projects in Australia for a diverse range of industry sectors.
If you are a passionate  Senior Data Engineer with experience on MS stack including MS Fabric beyond proof of concept, we encourage you to apply today via the link provided! Please note Sydney and Melbourne-based candidates with full time work rights only will be considered for this role as it is a requirement for this position and no sponsorship is on offer.

Candidates from all backgrounds are welcome to apply too. Please feel free to also send a CV to kgonzalez@siriustechnology.com.au, our Principal IT Consultant for Data, AI and Cyber, thanks.

About the Role:
Our client, a leading pharmaceutical manufacturing company based in Brisbane’s western suburbs, is seeking an experienced Control Systems Engineer - Data Historian to join their team for a causal ongoing position for 12 months, working Monday to Friday, 38 hours per week. This role plays a critical part in supporting manufacturing operations through reliable, compliant, and insightful industrial data systems.

In this role, you will design, implement, and maintain data historian systems that capture and analyse large volumes of time-series data from industrial environments (PLCs, SCADA, DCS). Your work will directly support operational efficiency, predictive maintenance, and regulatory compliance within a GMP-regulated setting.

NOTE: Applicants will be required to undertake a full medical assessment, drug screening and police check to be eligible for this role.

The successful candidate will be responsible but not limited to:
System Design & Implementation: Configure, deploy, and maintain historian platforms and data interfaces connected to manufacturing and packaging equipment.
Data Integration: Integrate historian systems with PLCs, SCADA, DCS, and enterprise systems such as LIMS and ERP.
Validation & Support: Write and execute qualification and validation protocols, and provide ongoing technical support.
Compliance: Ensure systems meet regulatory and quality requirements, including GMP and FDA 21 CFR Part 11.
Collaboration: Partner with automation, IT, engineering, quality, and operations teams to support data-driven decision-making.
Lifecycle Management: Support system upgrades, troubleshooting, and continuous improvement initiatives.

Skills & Experience:
The successful candidate must have:
A degree qualification in Engineering (or a related discipline)
Proven experience in a Data Historian, Control Sytems or Electrical Engineer, or similar role
Intermediate to advanced experience with PLCs, SCADA, and DCS systems
Strong understanding of GMP-regulated manufacturing environments
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
High attention to detail with strong time management and organisational skills

Apply!
If you think this could be the role for you then please apply now! Please send us a cover letter AND a detailed resume in WORD format (PDFs can be corrupted / not processed).  
Or, if you have any questions, please contact the Simon at simon.taylor@evolvescientific.com.au

Job Title: Senior Data Architect - 3 month Contract - $900 per day
Ad Text:
Senior Data Architect - Melbourne - $900 per Day - 3 Month Contract with a view to a permanent role - Own Data Strategy and Design - Immediate Start

 

Morgan Consulting are partnering with a leading organisation to appoint a Senior Data Architect on an initial 3 month contract with a view to a permanent role, paying $900 per day, with an immediate start available. This is a high impact role where you will own data architecture strategy and design, working within a mature Data Product team to support large scale, business critical data platforms. You will play a key role in shaping modern, cloud based data solutions within a fast moving, delivery focused environment.

 

About the Role

The Senior Data Architect is responsible for defining and driving the data architecture vision, strategy, and design across enterprise data platforms. Working closely with Data Engineers, Data Scientists and Product Managers, you will ensure data solutions are scalable, secure, and aligned to both technical and commercial objectives. This role is hands on and delivery focused, combining strategic architecture leadership with deep technical expertise across modern data engineering practices. You will operate within a Data Product team, helping translate complex business needs into clear, well designed data solutions.


What You Will Do

Data Architecture and Strategy


Own and define the data architecture strategy and target state designs across cloud based data platforms
Design scalable, secure, and high performing data architectures aligned to business and product outcomes
Provide architectural guidance across ingestion, transformation, storage, and consumption layers
Ensure architectural standards, patterns, and governance frameworks are clearly defined and applied

Data Engineering and Platforms


Work closely with Data Engineers to design and support ETL and ELT pipelines and data integration patterns
Contribute hands on expertise across Azure data services, Databricks, and modern cloud data tooling
Support CI CD practices for data platforms, pipelines, and infrastructure as code
Ensure data solutions are production ready, resilient, and optimised for performance and cost

Stakeholder and Product Collaboration


Partner with Data Product Managers and business stakeholders to translate requirements into scalable data designs
Provide clear architectural direction and communicate complex concepts in a pragmatic, outcome focused way
Act as a trusted advisor across technology and data teams

What You Will Bring

Proven experience as a Senior Data Architect or Lead Data Architect within complex environments
Strong background in data engineering alongside data architecture
Deep experience with Azure, including modern data services and cloud native architectures
Hands on expertise with ETL and ELT, CI CD, and Databricks
Strong understanding of modern data product operating models
Experience working in retail or customer centric environments highly regarded
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Ability to operate autonomously and deliver impact quickly in a contract setting
 

Why Join

$900 per day with immediate start
3 month contract with meaningful impact from day one
Opportunity to own data architecture strategy and design in a modern data environment
Work within a mature Data Product team using modern cloud and engineering practices
Fast paced, delivery focused culture with minimal red tape
 
If you are a Senior Data Architect with a strong data engineering background who thrives in fast paced environments and enjoys owning strategy as well as delivery, we would love to hear from you.
Apply now or reach out for a confidential conversation.

MMS is embarking on a major multi‑year technology transformation, rebuilding the core digital foundations that power some of Australia’s most recognised employee benefits and salary packaging brands. We’re modernising our platforms end‑to‑end and shifting to cloud‑native, scalable, event‑driven architectures that will set the business up for the future.

As part of this journey, you’ll join a team designing and delivering modern distributed systems; re‑engineering legacy workloads into contemporary cloud patterns; and solving complex integration challenges across secure, highly available enterprise platforms. It’s an opportunity to help shape a flagship transformation and contribute to the building of solutions for millions of customers.

If you enjoy building internal platforms, automating infrastructure, and making opinionated engineering decisions that scale, this role is for you.

As a Platform Engineer, you will design and operate the cloud platform that powers our financial services, enabling engineering teams to deliver secure, reliable software from local development through to mission-critical production.

This role is for a software/cloud platform engineer working with infrastructure automation and developer platforms (not a civil, structural, or mechanical engineering role).

How you’ll make a difference

You’ll be responsible for building and evolving the cloud platform that enables secure, scalable software delivery across the organisation.

You should enjoy writing code to automate infrastructure and platforms rather than manually configuring systems.

At MMS clear expectations help our people Be the Difference.  Your key responsibilities in this role will include: 

Design, implement and maintain Infrastructure as Code using Terraform

Design, build, and maintain CI/CD pipelines as code, enabling fully automated, reliable deployments across multiple environments

Develop reusable platform components and automation to improve developer experience and consistency

Implement platform engineering practices to standardise environments from development through production

Partner with application engineering teams to improve build pipelines, deployment patterns, and platform capabilities

Embed security, governance, and automation into the platform by design

Diagnose and resolve complex issues across cloud infrastructure, platform tooling, and deployment pipelines

 To be considered for this role you will have: 

Experience building reusable Terraform modules, platform tooling, or infrastructure abstractions that enable multiple development teams

Experience working in environments where infrastructure changes are delivered through pull requests and automated pipelines

Experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines (not just using them)

Proven hands-on experience in platform engineering, DevOps engineering, or cloud infrastructure engineering roles

Strong experience with Azure PaaS services

Experience designing cloud-native platforms supporting multiple engineering teams

Solid understanding of DevOps practices, Git-based workflows, and automated delivery

Ability to work closely with developers to improve build, deployment, and platform capabilities

Experience embedding security, compliance, and governance into cloud platforms

 What we can offer you:

Novated leasing benefits and discounts

12 weeks paid parental leave and access to our Parents Portal

Comprehensive learning and development opportunities to support your career growth

Sonder digital wellbeing platform, providing personalised support 24/7, plus annual flu vaccinations

Default Income Protection Insurance reimbursed for members of the MMS Default Super Fund

Exempt Employee Share Plan

Embracing our value of Everyone Matters we hold a collective commitment to foster an environment where all differences are valued and respected. 

We encourage individuals from all backgrounds including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, those caring for someone or living with a disability, LGBTQIA+ and culturally diverse applicants to apply.

We embrace hybrid working and welcome conversations about flexibility. 

We value the skills and attributes veterans can bring to our organisation.    

 Please note all successful candidates will undergo background checks (including criminal history and ASIC checks) and an NDIS Workers Screening Check if appropriate.  All information provided will be treated confidentially. 

 We acknowledge Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples as the Traditional Custodians of the lands where we live, learn and work.

 If you identify as a person living with disability and require adjustments to our recruitment process, please contact us at mmsgrouprecruitment@mmsg.com.au

Job Title: Lead Software Engineer - AI Platform Services
Ad Text:
Company Description

About SEEK

At SEEK, we serve a noble purpose: to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and to help organisations succeed.

By joining us, you’ll be part of a multi-national technology business that is far-reaching with a start-up working culture that focuses on a set of collaborative values and appreciates dynamic cultures. SEEK is a place where potential meets possibility – it’s where your career aspiration and our purpose can make great things happen.

Why join us?

Be part of a multi-national tech company with strong core values to help us solve complex challenges for our customers while building a flexible, exciting career – one that could take you anywhere.

We’re looking for people who thrive in dynamic teams and enjoy tackling regular new challenges in a large-scale, modern, and agile environment. As one of Asia Pacific’s largest tech companies, you’ll contribute to multi-lingual systems, solving high-impact problems to the benefit of millions of people within the region.

Be part of a fast-growing company with a strong culture and clear purpose, innovation and strong investment in responsible AI. SEEK offers you the opportunity to be involved in impactful work and be part of a great team. Expect a great investment in your career development with access to workshops, conferences and courses while you work alongside seasoned experts.

Job Description

About the role

You will lead the engineering function within the All the Ads team, part of SEEK's Pricing and Performance group. You will design and deliver AI/ML systems, data assets, and pipelines that help SEEK better understand hirers and job ads at scale. You will collaborate with data scientists and AI product managers across three problem domains, managing a team of engineers while driving technical excellence and rapid delivery. You will own system architecture, mature ML-Ops practices, and ensure smooth integration with partner teams across the business. Success means building high-performing cross-functional teams, meeting delivery roadmaps, and advancing AI capabilities that directly impact millions of users.

Key Responsibilities

Lead cross-functional team to apply latest AI/ML practices
Design system architecture that meets team objectives and SEEK standards
Provide technical leadership and mature ML-Ops practices
Foster rapid delivery culture; align team to roadmaps and timelines
Manage, coach, and grow engineers through line management
Collaborate with data science, product, and business stakeholders on key decisions
Oversee integration of backend services with partner teams
Qualifications

Skills and Experience

10+ years backend software engineering with strong technical skills
Proficient in Python and/or Java (required)
Strong experience with AWS cloud platform (required)
Strong SQL skills (required)
Experience with Spark/Databricks and ML engineering (desirable)
Proven delivery and people management experience
Additional Information

At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
The opportunity to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per financial year
Frequent events including sports days, year-end party, hackathon, and trivia

 

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us. Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those with eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

About us

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation. As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world's most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.
 

About the program

LMA has entered into an arrangement with BAE, Saab and CoA (MIWS) for the execution of all Combat System Integration work related to the Aegis Surface Combatant fleet. 

The agreement, called the Combat Systems Integration – Integrated Project Team (CSI-IPT) Collaboration Agreement or CCA forms a collaborative enterprise organisation to execute scopes of work across Hobart Class Destroyer (SEA4000 Phase 6 and other related Destroyer Capability Enhancement (DCE) upgrades) and the Hunter Class Frigates (HCFs - SEA5000 Phase 1). The CSI-IPT evolves the current independently executing programs such as SEA4000 Phase  6 and SEA5000 Phase 1 under an umbrella enterprise organisation formed as a program of programs. 




About the role

We are seeking a Combat Systems Engineer to join our Maritime Engineering team, supporting upgrades to the Hobart Class Destroyers under the Destroyer Capability Enhancement (DCE) program.

Working within the Combat Management System (CMS) domain team, you will contribute to the design, integration and delivery of combat system upgrades on in-service Aegis-equipped platforms. This is a great opportunity to apply your systems engineering skills on complex, real-world Defence capability. 

Key responsibilities include

Support systems engineering activities in the CMS domain including requirements analysis, design, integration and verification

Contribute to the development of system architectures, interfaces and design artefacts

Assist in the development and execution of Engineering Change Proposals (ECPs)

Provide technical support to integration, test and production activities

Collaborate with stakeholders across Defence, industry partners and internal teams

Contribute to requirements traceability, system documentation and engineering frameworks

Support risk, safety, security and supportability considerations across the system lifecycle




About you

You are a capable systems engineer with experience working on complex Defence or engineering programs. You enjoy solving technical problems, working across teams, and contributing to the delivery of integrated systems. 

Skills and experience

Experience across the systems engineering lifecycle (requirements, design, integration, V&V)

Exposure to Aegis-equipped naval platforms, combat systems or complex integrated systems

Experience contributing to system design, documentation and engineering change activities

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

Ability to work with multiple stakeholders in a multidisciplinary environment

Good communication skills with the ability to present technical information clearly

An Australian NV-1 security clearance and ability to access/handle Export Controlled technology, data and information

Desirable

Experience in integration, test and evaluation of Defence systems

Familiarity with PLM tools (e.g. Windchill) or requirements tools

Knowledge of C4ISREW or maritime combat system domains

Why join us

Work on a high-profile naval Defence program

Gain exposure to complex combat system integration

Be part of a collaborative and experienced engineering team




How We Reward and Support You

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

A variety of flexible work options

Access to our online discounts program offering immediate discounts or cashback

A variety of leave options including Purchase Leave to support you and your family  

Additional benefits such as Annual Flu Vaccinations, Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs


Special Requirements

Applicants must be eligible to obtain and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance and employment may be contingent on the provision of further personal information including citizenship to ensure compliance with international export control legislation and regulations.

A Veteran-friendly Workplace

Veterans are encouraged to apply. We highly value the unique skills and experiences that veterans and transitioning service members can contribute to our team. We are proud to be recognised as a Veteran Friendly Employer by the Australian Government.

Equal Opportunity Statement

Lockheed Martin Australia supports Equal Employment Opportunity and values diversity in the workplace by creating a place of employment that is, free of unlawful discrimination and is harassment free, fair, ethical, inclusive, and values the contribution of all its employees. By leveraging our employees' unique talents and experiences, we will deliver innovative, affordable solutions and unparalleled customer value.

As part of our endeavours to increase participation of Indigenous Australians, we welcome applications from all people, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are selected for this role, we would be happy to discuss any accommodations that will help you to be successful.

The Role

Join a high-performing Security Operations team responsible for strengthening detection capabilities and improving incident response through automation. This role combines threat detection, digital forensics, and SOAR to reduce cyber risk and uplift security maturity. You'll work closely with offensive security, IT, and risk teams to continuously enhance detection and response capabilities.

Key Responsibilities

Develop, deploy, and tune SIEM correlation rules and detection use cases
Enhance detection capability, tools, and processes
Collaborate on red/purple team exercises to identify gaps
Build and maintain SOAR playbooks and automation workflows
Automate incident response processes using PowerShell and Python
Drive continuous improvement through automation and AI
Support incident response, threat investigations, and forensics

Skills & Experience

Strong experience in threat detection and SIEM use case development]
Hands-on scripting (PowerShell, Python) and SOAR playbooks
Solid understanding of MITRE ATT&CK and
Attacker techniques
Experience in SOC, incident response, and detection engineering
Exposure to SIEM/SOAR tools (e.g. Splunk ES, Splunk SOAR)
Knowledge of cloud, infrastructure, and enterprise environments

Benefits

Competitive daily rate
Work on advanced security tooling and automation
Exposure to enterprise-scale cyber operations
Collaborative and high-performing team environment
About Toll People

Toll People are Australia's most recognised provider of Transport, Logistics, Supply Chain and Warehouse staff. Whilst we service the Toll Group, we also service hundreds of external clients and partners, offering complete recruitment solutions from permanent and executive recruitment, business support recruitment to flexible labour and award-winning training and development.
If you're interested in this role, please click on 'Apply'. Should you have any questions please call Muna on 03 8369 2400
Toll Group does not accept any unsolicited resume referrals from Recruitment Agencies and will not pay any placement fees relating to such unsolicited resume referrals.

All applicants must be entitled to work in Australia and be prepared to undergo a criminal history check, pre-employment medical and/or drug & alcohol testing as required.

Toll embraces and celebrates a variety of cultures. We continue to build a business that reflects the values of equality, built on the knowledge and understanding that everyone is welcome including the First Nations Peoples, and those of all ages, genders and abilities.

Job Title: Lead Product Manager – Melbourne – $200,000 - Gain AI – Amazing Culture
Ad Text:
Lead Product Manager – Melbourne – $200,000 - Lead a Product Team – Productionised AI Features – Strong Career Development – Amazing Culture

Morgan Consulting are partnering with an exciting and forward thinking organisation to appoint a Lead Product Manager to lead a high performing product team and drive the strategy, vision, and execution across a critical suite of business critical applications. This is a standout opportunity to deliver AI enabled, production ready features, influence enterprise‑scale products, and shape both customer and operational experiences. This role offers strong career development within an organisation that empowers its people, moves quickly, and fosters a collaborative, inclusive, and high trust culture with minimal red tape.

We are looking for a true product specialist who is equally comfortable with the data and engineering foundations of modern products as they are with the craft of product management itself. Someone who brings energy, curiosity, and genuine passion for product, and who thrives on turning complexity into clear, valuable outcomes. You will have strong stakeholder management skills, able to confidently balance maintaining and improving existing products while also driving the introduction of new capabilities, including AI enabled features, to deliver meaningful impact.

About the Role

The Lead Product Manager is responsible for leading and developing a team of six Product Specialists while owning the strategic direction, vision, and delivery of a portfolio of applications that underpin core operations. This role blends people leadership with hands on product strategy, working closely with engineering, data, design, and business stakeholders to deliver high impact, customer‑centred solutions. A key focus of the role is supporting the delivery of AI driven and productionised features, ensuring products are scalable, reliable, and aligned with both customer needs and commercial outcomes. You will play a pivotal role in fostering a culture of collaboration, innovation, and outcome driven delivery across the product organisation.

What You Will Do

Product Strategy & Delivery
Own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap across a suite of applications supporting large scale operations.
Guide prioritisation decisions to balance customer value, commercial outcomes, and technical sustainability.
Lead the delivery of AI enabled and data driven features, ensuring solutions are production ready and scalable.
Partner closely with engineering teams to ensure products are built, operated, and continuously improved using modern development practices.
Drive strong alignment between business objectives and product outcomes.
Leadership & Team Development
Lead, mentor, and inspire a team of six Product Managers, supporting their growth and capability development.
Foster a high performance culture centred on accountability, psychological safety, and continuous improvement.
Set clear expectations and provide coaching on product discovery, delivery, and storytelling.
Stakeholder & Customer Engagement
Act as a trusted product leader across the business, engaging stakeholders at all levels.
Communicate product vision, strategy, and progress through clear narratives and compelling storytelling.
Collaborate with internal users and customers to ensure solutions deliver meaningful and measurable value.
Innovation & Ways of Working
Champion experimentation, learning, and innovation across the product function.
Promote modern product management practices, including outcome based roadmaps and evidence driven decision making.
Work collaboratively across product, engineering, data, and design to deliver seamless, end to end experiences.
What You Will Bring
Proven experience in leading a product management team, owning complex digital products and platforms.
Demonstrated experience leading and developing high performing product teams.
Strong understanding of how modern applications are designed, built, operated, and maintained.
Hands on experience working directly alongside software engineers and technology teams.
Exceptional communication, stakeholder engagement, and storytelling skills.
Ability to balance strategic leadership with pragmatic, hands‑on execution.
Preferred Experience
Experience working in large‑scale, multi‑system or customer centric environments.
Familiarity with agile delivery, modern product operating models, and scaled product organisations.
Why Join
Opportunity to lead and shape production ready AI powered products at scale.
Strong career development and leadership progression opportunities.
Inclusive, people first culture where individuals are empowered to make a real impact.
Commitment to diversity, fairness, and equal opportunity across all backgrounds and life stages.
If you are a Lead Product Manager who blends deep product craft with a strong understanding of data and engineering, thrives in fast paced environments, enjoys leading and growing high‑performing teams, and is driven to deliver impactful products that matter, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now or reach out for a confidential conversation.

We are seeking a skilled and experienced Security Consultant focused on SIEM, Security Automation and Response (SOAR), and overall SOC technology capabilities to join our team. The ideal candidate will possess a deep understanding of SIEM/SOAR technologies and related solutions, with expertise in designing, implementing, and optimising security automation and orchestration strategies for our clients.

The SIEM/SOAR Consultant will work closely with clients to understand their security needs, architect tailored security tool plans and deployments, and ensure successful project delivery.

Responsibilities:

1.      Client Engagement and Relationship Management:

·       Engage with clients to understand their security requirements, pain points, and business objectives.

·       Develop and maintain strong client relationships, ensuring customer satisfaction and driving repeat business.

2.      SIEM/SOAR Architecture and Design:

·       Design and architect SOAR solutions based on clients’ unique security needs and infrastructure.

·       Suggest and evaluate different SIEM/SOAR technologies, comparing and contrasting solutions for clients.

·       Create detailed technical documentation, including architecture diagrams, implementation plans, and configuration guides.

3.      Deployment and Configuration:

·       Lead the deployment of SOC platforms, ensuring adherence to best practices and security standards.

·       Correctly size tools to vendor specifications to meet clients’ needs, including specific vendor add-ons and their optimisation to meet customer demands.

·       Design the integration of SIEM/SOAR platforms with other security tools, such as firewalls, intrusion detection systems, and endpoint protection platforms.

·       Assist with hands-on coding of these tools when required.

4.      Playbook and Workflow Development:

·       Develop and maintain playbooks, workflows, and automation scripts using SIEM/SOAR tools.

·       Collaborate with clients to refine and enhance playbooks based on their specific use cases and requirements.

5.      Incident Response and Orchestration:

·       Assist clients in developing and refining incident response strategies using SIEM/SOAR automation and orchestration capabilities.

·       Support clients in managing and coordinating security incidents, leveraging SIEM/SOAR investigation and orchestration features.

6.      Training and Knowledge Transfer:

·       Deliver training sessions and workshops to clients' security teams, covering tool features, best practices, and customization.

·       Facilitate knowledge transfer and capacity building within client organisations.

7.      Project Management:

·       Manage multiple concurrent projects, ensuring timely delivery within budget and scope.

·       Collaborate with internal teams, including pre-sales, professional services, and support, to ensure seamless project execution.

·       Identify project gaps and address them as required for successful completion

8.      Continuous Improvement and Innovation:

·       Stay up to date with the latest SIEM/SOAR technologies, industry trends, and best practices.

·       Contribute to the continuous improvement of our SOAR consulting services and methodologies.

Qualifications:

1.      Education and Certifications:

a.      Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.

b.      Relevant SOAR certifications (e.g., IBM Resilient, Demisto(XSOAR), XSIAM or vendor-specific certifications) highly desirable.

c.      Relevant SIEM certifications (e.g., administrator or architect level) in SIEM vendor technologies.

d.      Relevant Security Certification  (e.g., GIAC, CISSP) highly desirable.

2.      Experience:

·       Minimum of 3 years’ experience in SOC tools consulting, with additional experience as a user, administrator, or operator.

·       Proven track record of designing, implementing, and optimising SOAR solutions.

3.      Technical Skills:

·       Extensive knowledge of SIEM/SOAR technologies and platforms, such as IBM Qradar, IBM Resilient, Splunk/Phantom or Demisto (Palo Alto Cortex XSOAR).

·       Proficiency in automation tools and scripting languages (e.g. Python, PowerShell, Bash).

·       Strong understanding of security orchestration, automation, and response (SOAR) concepts and best practices.

·       Familiarity with other security solutions, such as incident response platforms, threat intelligence platforms, and Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) systems.

About the opportunity

As we continue to grow across Australia, we’re seeking Configuration Management professionals to support complex engineering programs across our Integrated Air & Missile Defence, Maritime and Aeronautics portfolios.

You’ll work in a multidisciplinary environment, helping maintain system configuration, product baselines and engineering change control ensuring accuracy, traceability and compliance across the full lifecycle.

The Hospital:

The Royal Children's Hospital's (RCH) Vision is "A world where all kids thrive".

RCH is a cornerstone member of the Melbourne Children's Campus, partnering with Murdoch Children's Research Institute, The University of Melbourne Department of Paediatrics and The Royal Children's Hospital Foundation.

RCH has cared for the children and young people of Victoria for more than 150 years since it was founded in 1870. A full range of paediatrics and adolescent health services are provided plus tertiary and quaternary care for the most critically ill and medically complex patients in Victoria, Tasmania, southern NSW and other states around Australia and overseas. The Hospital has more than 6,000 staff, a budget of $850M, 12 wards and 350 beds. Annually, the RCH has 300,000+ Specialist Clinic appointments, 90,000+ Emergency Department presentations and 20,000 elective surgeries.

The RCH is committed to the Child Safe Standards.

Universal Field Robots – A Sandvik Company

Mechatronics Engineer– Eagle Farm, Qld.




About the opportunity:

This is an exciting opportunity to join UFR as a Mechatronics Engineer, where you will design, develop, and integrate advanced electrical and software systems that enable autonomous operation of mobile mining equipment. Reporting to the Mechatronics Engineering Manager, you will play a key role in delivering innovative automation solutions that support Sandvik’s “Zero-Entry Mine” strategy and enhance safety and productivity in mining environments.

In this role, you will lead electrical design for mobile machinery, develop and troubleshoot PLC software, and integrate hardware and communication protocols to ensure seamless system functionality. You will also support on-site and off-site field trials, diagnose and resolve technical issues, and maintain compliance with safety standards and engineering processes.

Beyond day-to-day responsibilities, you will contribute to cross-functional projects, drive continuous improvement, and help shape cutting-edge automation technology that positions UFR as a leader in OEM-agnostic solutions for the mining industry.




Responsibilities:

Design and review wiring harnesses for mobile equipment, ensuring compliance with UFR and industry standards.

Select, integrate, and configure hardware components for autonomous systems.

Develop and troubleshoot PLC software for mobile machinery.

Implement and maintain communication protocols such as CAN, J1939, and CANOpen.

Conduct code reviews and manage version control to ensure quality and traceability.

Support field trials by creating test plans, diagnosing issues, and implementing fixes.

Travel to operational sites across Australia and internationally for product development and commissioning.

Maintain accurate documentation for installations, modifications, and deployed systems.

Collaborate in project scoping and design, ensuring deliverables meet timelines and quality standards.

Uphold integrity, accountability, and compliance with safety and professional standards.




About you

You are a degree‑qualified engineer in Mechatronics, Electrical, Software, or a related discipline, with a strong interest in automation and advanced control systems. You have at least 2 years of experience designing and integrating electrical and software solutions for mobile machinery or robotics, and you’re comfortable working across PLC programming, communication protocols, and system troubleshooting. If you bring significantly more experience, or strengths in some (but not all) of these areas, we’d still love to hear from you.

Your technical expertise is complemented by strong problem-solving skills and the ability to collaborate effectively in cross-functional teams. You thrive in dynamic environments, enjoy tackling complex challenges, and are comfortable supporting field trials both on-site and remotely.

You have exposure to mining automation, mobile machinery or heavy equipment control systems. Familiarity with CAN protocols (e.g. J1939/CANopen), networking fundamentals and functional safety (e.g. ISO 13849/IEC 61508) are desirable but not essential.

Above all, you are proactive, detail-oriented, and committed to delivering high-quality engineering solutions that drive innovation and support UFR’s mission.

 

What we offer

Training and development opportunities, from extensive internal programs to contributions towards external studies.

Company-funded paid parental leave, with superannuation contributions during the leave period.

Sandvik Wellness Program to support and enhance your health and wellbeing

Comprehensive Employee Benefits Program, including salary sacrifice options, and a Length of Service Recognition program.

 

Who we are:

Are you a passionate technologist who thrives on solving complex problems, supporting diverse business needs, and driving best practices? Join UFR, now proudly operating under Sandvik, where we develop innovative autonomous robotic applications that are transforming industries like mining, agriculture, and construction.

Our robots are built with advanced sensing and computing technology to make outdoor work smarter, safer, and more efficient. As part of the Sandvik Group, a global leader in mining and rock solutions, we combine the agility of a tech company with the strength and stability of a global organisation. We are committed to sustainability, inclusion, and building a better future through engineering. If you're ready to make a real impact in a forward-thinking and inclusive environment, we'd love to hear from you.




For any enquiries on this role please reach out to Human Resources Operations Specialist - Alexis McKechnie at alexis.mckechnie_c@sandvik.com.
